channel.title: Comments on: Big Sky Brewing’s Moose Drool Brown Ale – Ep. 63
channel.description: Beer America TV is the first beer web site dedicated to the specialty craft brewing world with video on industry experts and insiders plus more!

item.title: By: Hanger18
item.dc:creator: Hanger18
item.description: Hey guys, a little off topic but just my two cents I feel like cans are an awesome idea for packaging look at Oskar Blues brewery in Lyons Colorado. They have had great success from canning beer, 100% growth for the past 5 years! It can really help with getting your name out there cause there aren't a ton of craft brewers/micro brewers out there doing it. However that being said, as you stated earlier there is that stigma associated with beer in cans, even though in almost every way a can it better. I will say I do prefer to have my beer in bottles, but as far a marketing and getting your product noticed by the larger consumer base ie. the entry craft beer drinkers and the macro drinkers it a great sttention grabber.
item.content:encoded: Hey guys, a little off topic but just my two cents I feel like cans are an awesome idea for packaging look at Oskar Blues brewery in Lyons Colorado. They have had great success from canning beer, 100% growth for the past 5 years! It can really help with getting your name out there cause there aren’t a ton of craft brewers/micro brewers out there doing it. However that being said, as you stated earlier there is that stigma associated with beer in cans, even though in almost every way a can it better. I will say I do prefer to have my beer in bottles, but as far a marketing and getting your product noticed by the larger consumer base ie. the entry craft beer drinkers and the macro drinkers it a great sttention grabber.

item.title: By: andrewt248
item.dc:creator: andrewt248
item.description: I love brown ales, especially American brown ales. I know you guys are fans of Bell's Brewery beers; you guys should try the alliterative Bell's Best Brown. WOW what a complex brown ale!!! It's like chocolate covered dark fruits. Delicious! I'll be looking for the Moose Drool.
item.content:encoded: I love brown ales, especially American brown ales. I know you guys are fans of Bell’s Brewery beers; you guys should try the alliterative Bell’s Best Brown. WOW what a complex brown ale!!! It’s like chocolate covered dark fruits. Delicious! I’ll be looking for the Moose Drool.

item.title: By: Beer Obsessed
item.dc:creator: Beer Obsessed
item.description: Wow, didn't realize Moose Drool was being canned! I love this beer. We have had it in the SF Bay Area for a while now. Hope we get the cans soon!
item.content:encoded: Wow, didn’t realize Moose Drool was being canned! I love this beer. We have had it in the SF Bay Area for a while now. Hope we get the cans soon!

item.title: By: Wistful
item.dc:creator: Wistful
item.description:
Pinkerton, I really like your insights but you hurt me with your comments about Newcastle. Newkie Brown is "pabst" in my house. It is my cheap, everyday brew that provides me with some level of ale drinking when I am saving $ towards craft brews.
Newcastle can't hold a candle to Brooklyn, Highland, Dogfish, New Belgium, etc but it is still a hell of a lot better than BMC.
item.content:encoded:
Pinkerton, I really like your insights but you hurt me with your comments about Newcastle. Newkie Brown is “pabst” in my house. It is my cheap, everyday brew that provides me with some level of ale drinking when I am saving $ towards craft brews.

Newcastle can’t hold a candle to Brooklyn, Highland, Dogfish, New Belgium, etc but it is still a hell of a lot better than BMC.

item.title: By: Paul
item.dc:creator: Paul
item.description: For any real craft beer drinker, this is a great one to try. I really was pleasantly surprised on how good this beer was. Coming up in a few weeks, we'll have a few more Big Sky beers to share. Thanks for the comment!
item.content:encoded:
For any real craft beer drinker, this is a great one to try. I really was pleasantly surprised on how good this beer was. Coming up in a few weeks, we’ll have a few more Big Sky beers to share.
Thanks for the comment!

item.title: By: nate
item.dc:creator: nate
item.description:
I too have been wanting to try moose drool...can't get it in Northwestern OH, so for now I'll have to take your word for it.
Nice site, by the way.
item.content:encoded:
I too have been wanting to try moose drool…can’t get it in Northwestern OH, so for now I’ll have to take your word for it.

Nice site, by the way.
